content.problemStatement:
You are given a string consisting of lowercase English alphabets. Your task is to determine if it's possible to split this string into three non-empty parts (substrings) where one of these parts is a substring of both remaining parts.

For example, in string "abcabab", we can split it into:


First part: "abc"
Second part: "ab"
Third part: "abc"
Here "ab" is a substring of both "abc" and "abc".
**Note:**


All three parts must be non-empty
Parts must be formed by continuous characters from the original string
The parts should use all characters of the original string in order
The order of splitting should maintain the original sequence of characters

Input Format

First line contains a single integer TTT (1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100) - number of test cases
Each of the next TTT lines contains:

A string str (3≤∣str∣≤10003 \leq |str| \leq 10003≤∣str∣≤1000) consisting only of lowercase English alphabets




Output Format

For each test case, print "YES" if such a split is possible, "NO" otherwise

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

String = "abcabab"
Can be split as: "abc" + "ab" + "abc"
"ab" is substring of both "abc"

Test Case 2:

String = "aaa"
Can be split as: "a" + "a" + "a"
"a" is substring of both "a"

Test Case 3:

String = "abcd"
No possible split exists where one part is substring of other two

Test Case 4:

String = "aabaab"
Can be split as: "aab" + "aa" + "b"
"aa" is substring of "aab"
